quote_of_day.text:
>let us destroy people lives over jokes
We make sadistic jokes here every day. Same with all the people on twitter and reddit.
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of existential dread hovered over the board, as Anon pondered the coping mechanisms of baunajeets. This brought scattered showers of dark humor and absurdist remarks.

A gentle drizzle of art requests fell across the board, with Anon soliciting drawings from fellow users.

Nearby, a brief but intense heat wave of nostalgia swept through a discussion on old imageboard projects, with one user sharing and discussing long-abandoned work.

Meanwhile, a cold front of intergenerational dismay moved in, as Anon lamented the rapid changes in India over two generations.

A high-pressure ridge of hedonism formed nearby, with another Anon declaring that spending on female company was well worth the cost.

articles.title: Anon ponders baunajeets' coping mechanisms
articles.body:
Anon started a thread with the post 'How baunajeets cope'. One anon responded with a remark about manlets gifting their wives to 'tall chads', suggesting that manlets could never make their wives feel the same way.

This anon also made a personal comment about the OP's wife. Another anon shared their own experience, stating they are only 5'11 and find it difficult to cope with the prevalence of taller individuals.

A respondent claimed to be 4'10, joking that they are 'mentally still a kid'. One anon humorously noted that turning gay or bisexual might be a coping mechanism for not getting girls.

In response to the 4'10 claim, another anon made a comment about being legally considered a dwarf and potentially eligible for a disability pension.

An anon shared their personal details, stating they are 21 and will turn 22 in September, and identified as OBC. A brief exchange occurred about an image, with one anon saying 'Thik h na yaar'.

Some anons engaged in banter, with one saying 'ok pajeet its end of your larp' and another calling someone 'Based' and inquiring about being a 'baunachad'. Anons also inquired about each other's personal details, including caste and age.

One anon boasted about being 5'5 and having a girlfriend. Some anons directed hostile comments at others, including 'kys manlet'.

articles.title: Anon Requests Drawings on Indiachan
articles.body:
Anon started the thread by saying "Send draw requests".

One anon asked for a drawing of "fagyush is blowing chodiji while they are floating on a universe which is based on a lotus", and to include a note that all pajeetchans were made by the same entity using different names.

Another anon requested a drawing of "rakulcel summarising him and his life situation in just a frame". A third anon asked for a drawing of a character from a different context altogether.

The conversation took a turn when an anon claimed to know the original creator of the first jaggu and stated they had left Indiachan.

This was met with questions about the creator's identity and their involvement in other projects.

Anon continued to make drawing requests, including one for "Geeta beating the shit out of a sarkaari babu with a rusted Sariya". Others asked for drawings of sexy girls and characters from various contexts.

The thread also included some discussion about the identity of the original creator of certain content and whether they were still active on the board.

articles.title: Anon shares and discusses old imageboard projects
articles.body:
Anon posted never-released photos from their projects, starting with one called 'khichdi', which was previously named 'xbharat' and accessed via an IP address. The original poster mentioned that 'xbharat' had better posts, including porn.

Another anon shared that they hadn't worked on anything for months but were inspired by a post on an imageboard about making one, which led to a nostalgia trip.

This anon recalled loving Windows phones, which they couldn't own, and created a quick minimum viable product (MVP) with a similar aesthetic and animations.

The original poster discussed their experience with aesthetics criticism, stating that people complained about the design, leading them to try using libraries. They joked about their motto at the time: 'overpromise, underdeliver'.

One anon suggested a collaboration between the original poster and another user, praising the creativity of the other's ideas, methods, and design.

The conversation also touched on the topic of search engine optimization (SEO) and the decision to look into pure HTML websites without single-page applications (SPAs).

Anons exchanged comments on their experiences with imageboards, including dealing with spam and shifting focus towards text-based content.

The thread concluded with the original poster requesting that the admin delete the thread in 48 hours and another anon asking for a link to one of the projects mentioned.

articles.title: Anon laments change in India over two generations
articles.body: Anon opened the thread saying, "Mera desh itni jaldi badal gaya yaaro contrast toh dekho do peedi mein kya se kya hogya" Another anon responded with, "dadi must have been a good fuck back in the day". A respondent claimed, "Fucking dalit taking creepshots". Anon also joked, "I am ajit doval you are under arrest".

articles.title: Spending on female company
articles.body: Anon claimed that hanging out with girls is worth the spending. No one replied to the post.
